objective: Audit lateral-boundary forcing fidelity as the root of the Switzerland/Gotthard d01 h36 strong-flow dry-mass venting (depth-8 h37 excess outflux -26.5 Pa/cell/h vs CPU -74.5; CPU own residual +5).

1_venting_is_real_instantaneous_outflux_.evidence: Using ONLY the end-state (h37) flux, BOTH CPU and GPU close: CPU resid(endflux)=+1.1, GPU resid(endflux)=+4.5. The -18 'non-closure' in the trapezoidal budget is an artifact of averaging the shared CPU h36 flux (+78.6) with the GPU h37 flux (+123.5). GPU instantaneous h37 outflux 123.5 vs CPU 70.4 == +53 Pa/cell/h excess.
1_venting_is_real_instantaneous_outflux_.verdict: GPU is internally mass-consistent; the venting is genuine excess lateral OUTFLUX, not an interior mass-sink discretization error.

2_excess_is_v_wind_meridional_NS_faces_d.reading: Net excess -26.5 is dominated by the S/N (v-flux) faces: too much northward outflow at N (+40), too little inflow at S (-41). The strong N-S Foehn jet is systematically too fast in the GPU.
2_excess_is_v_wind_meridional_NS_faces_d.depth_invariance: h37 per-cell excess is ~-26 at depth 4/8/12/16/20/24 -> the v-bias pervades the whole interior, not a thin perimeter ring.

3_spec_zone_is_perfectly_pinned.evidence: GPU-CPU normal-wind bias at b_dist=0 (outermost spec row/col) is EXACTLY 0.000 on all four faces. The bias appears at the relaxation zone (b_dist=1,2,3) and grows monotonically inward.
3_spec_zone_is_perfectly_pinned.verdict: spec_bdyupdate / spec-zone hard-set is correct; the defect is in the RELAXATION lane and the interior jet it fails to hold.

4_relaxation_strength_governs_the_ventin.reading: Tripling NORMAL_BDY_RELAX_STRENGTH (20->60) cuts h37 excess 19% and collapses the N-face venting (+40->-4), but OVERSHOOTS other faces (W +3->-17) and does NOT persist (h38 -21.5->-22.1). Strength is a calibration knob pulling toward a BIASED target, not a root fix.

5_ROOT_the_normal_momentum_target_LEAF_i.code: src/gpuwrf/integration/d02_replay.py:load_wrfbdy_boundary_leaves -> _wrfbdy_total_mass_strips (line 1713) computes the uncoupling mass term ONCE from the IC (h36) dry mass mu_total and freezes it for the whole forecast. The wrfbdy COUPLED momentum V_BYS is uncoupled as v = V_BYS/(c1*mass_IC+c2)*msfvx (line 1645-1646, 1717-1724).
5_ROOT_the_normal_momentum_target_LEAF_i.wrf_truth: WRF couples/uncouples with the CURRENT (time-evolving) column mass (dyn_em/module_big_step_utilities_em.F::couple; module_bc_em.F spec/relax operate on the coupled momentum at the live mass).
5_ROOT_the_normal_momentum_target_LEAF_i.measured_leaf_target_error_h37: v_leaf low by ~0.156 m/s at N, ~0.053 at S (the domain lost ~1.8 kPa of mass by h37, so mass_TRUE/mass_IC=0.989 and the static-IC uncouple inflates the implied velocity denominator).
5_ROOT_the_normal_momentum_target_LEAF_i.why_it_is_the_residual_root: At saturated relax strength (60) the per-face flux is pulled to the leaf target but CANNOT reach CPU truth (residual -21.5, fw -17/fe +11/fs -19/fn -4) because the leaf TARGET itself is mass-biased. The relaxation faithfully tracks a wrong target.
5_ROOT_the_normal_momentum_target_LEAF_i.sign_caveat: The static-mass leaf bias is NEGATIVE on all faces (mass dropped). Its direct per-face venting sign is MIXED (W+S vent, E+N anti-vent), so it is NOT the FULL -26.5 at default strength (where the dominant term is the relax being too WEAK to hold the over-driven jet). It IS the limiting residual once the relax is strong enough to impose the target.

hypothesis_ledger.H1_spec_zone_forcing_uv_mass_coupling: PARTIAL-ROOT: spec ring-0 is exact (b=0 bias 0.000). The COUPLED normal-momentum target (normal_bdy_work_target_u/v) re-couples with stage mass but is built from a leaf uncoupled with STATIC IC mass -> mass-biased target = the residual venting root (finding 5).
hypothesis_ledger.H2_relaxation_strength_F(n): CONFIRMED-MECHANISM, NOT-ROOT: the WRF lbc_fcx_gcx weights are reproduced exactly (boundary_apply._wrf_relax_weights == module_bc_em.F:1318-1319). The deviation is the NORMAL_BDY_RELAX_STRENGTH=20 amplifier (WRF=1), a non-faithful proxy for WRF's coupled-tendency-folded-into-ru_tend cadence. Strength governs venting (sweep) but pulls toward the biased target.
hypothesis_ledger.H3_dynamics_tendency_leak_into_spec_zone: NOT-DEMONSTRATED as driver: WRF trims u/v PGF + rk_addtend_dry to exclude spec_zone (module_small_step_em.F:735-741, module_em.F:1671-1675); the GPU edge-masks Coriolis (rk_addtend_dry.py:432-457). The bias is domain-pervasive (interior +0.075 U/+0.047 V), inconsistent with a thin spec-zone diffusion leak alone. Not the primary signal.
hypothesis_ledger.H4_mapfactor_metric_in_normal_mass_flux: CONSISTENT, not the isolated root: the same msfuy/msfvx and (c1*muf+c2) coupling appear in both the budget kernel and the leaf uncouple; the error is the MASS (IC vs live), not the map factor.

recommended_fix.primary: Make the wrfbdy normal-momentum forcing use the LIVE column mass, not the frozen IC mass. Most WRF-faithful: keep the wrfbdy boundary momentum COUPLED (V_BYS as-is) and drive normal_bdy_work_target_u/v so small_step_finish reconstructs to v = (msf*V_BYS_uncoupled_at_LIVE_mass ...); concretely feed the coupled leaf and uncouple inside the per-stage target builder with prep.muv (the live stage mass already available there) instead of pre-uncoupling with static IC mass in load_wrfbdy_boundary_leaves.
recommended_fix.secondary: Once the target is live-mass-correct, the NORMAL_BDY_RELAX_STRENGTH amplifier should be re-derivable toward the WRF nominal (1) since the leaf will be reachable; verify it no longer needs 20x.
recommended_fix.do_not: Do NOT just raise the strength constant (overshoots per-face, non-persistent, not WRF-faithful).
